title: Enlargement: Albania and EU provisionally close negotiations on science and research, education and culture, and external relations
content: Today, the ninth meeting of the Accession Conference with Albania provisionally closed the first negotiating chapters on science and research (25), education and culture (26), and external relations (30).
snippet: Today, the ninth meeting of the Accession Conference with Albania provisionally closed the first negotiating chapters on science and research (25), education and culture (26), and external relations

title: Enlargement: EU and Moldova start negotiations on external relations policies
content: Today, the EU and the Republic of Moldova took another important step in the accession process by opening negotiations on the so-called ‘external relations’ cluster.
snippet: Today, the EU and the Republic of Moldova took another important step in the accession process by opening negotiations on the so-called ‘external relations’ cluster.

title: Enlargement: EU and Montenegro close accession negotiations on competition policy and customs union
content: Today, the 28th meeting of the Accession Conference with Montenegro provisionally closed accession negotiations on competition policy (chapter 8) and customs union (chapter 29).
snippet: Today, the 28th meeting of the Accession Conference with Montenegro provisionally closed accession negotiations on competition policy (chapter 8) and customs union (chapter 29).

title: Have your say on the progress review of the Drone Strategy 2.0
content: The Commission is inviting citizens and stakeholders alike to support the progress review of the Drone Strategy 2.0 by sharing their views on the future of Europe's drone ecosystem.
snippet: The Commission is inviting citizens and stakeholders alike to support the progress review of the Drone Strategy 2.0 by sharing their views on the future of Europe's drone ecosystem.

title: National Building Renovation Plans - the Commission’s first assessment
content: In its assessment, the Commission looked at the level of ambition of national targets, policies and measures planned, the adequacy of funding as well as the inclusiveness of public consultation.
snippet: In its assessment, the Commission looked at the level of ambition of national targets, policies and measures planned, the adequacy of funding as well as the inclusiveness of public consultation.

title: REPowerEU auction revenue target reached
content: The auction calendar has been adjusted following the suspension of the auctioning of the second leg of emission allowances allocated to the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF).
snippet: The auction calendar has been adjusted following the suspension of the auctioning of the second leg of emission allowances allocated to the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF).

title: Greece is looking at introducing micro-credentials in CVET
content: The EQAVET Greek National Reference Point organised a peer review on the link between continuing vocational education and training and micro-credentials to assess an initiative on the potential introduction of micro-credentials in CVET.

title: Fishing cooperative revives Adriatic smoking traditions
content: The Adria fishing cooperative in Tribunj has turned the Croatian tradition of smoked fish and salted sardines into a growing business that enriches the region’s culinary offerings.
snippet: The Adria fishing cooperative in Tribunj has turned the Croatian tradition of smoked fish and salted sardines into a growing business that enriches the region’s culinary offerings.

title: Europeans concerned about child safety online as new report publishes recommendations
content: EU survey shows Europeans want action on child safety online and new special panel report delivers recommendations
snippet: EU survey shows Europeans want action on child safety online and new special panel report delivers recommendations

title: New report published: Building Food 2040: stakeholder input for the new EU Strategic Research & Innovation Agenda for Food Systems
content: A new report summarises feedback from an EU-wide stakeholder consultation to inform Food 2040, the new EU Strategic Research and Innovation Agenda for Food Systems.
snippet: A new report summarises feedback from an EU-wide stakeholder consultation to inform Food 2040, the new EU Strategic Research and Innovation Agenda for Food Systems.

title: Evaluation confirms Nitrates Directive is effective and relevant, but identifies scope for smarter implementation
content: A Commission assessment also identifies scope for smarter implementation of the decades-old law.
snippet: A Commission assessment also identifies scope for smarter implementation of the decades-old law.

title: Commission upgrades import monitoring mechanism
content: To respond to growing imbalances in global trade, the European Commission is upgrading its import monitoring toolbox by reconfiguring its ‘import barometer’, which gathers up-to-date import data and was originally established in 2025.

title: European Skills High-Level Board calls for stronger business-education links to boost Europe’s skills development
content: The European Skills High-Level Board, presented by its Chair, Ylva Johansson, handed over its first conclusions. The conclusions call for regional and sectoral skills ecosystems to reskill adults faster and closer to where jobs actually are.
snippet: The European Skills High-Level Board, presented by its Chair, Ylva Johansson, handed over its first conclusions. The conclusions call for regional and sectoral skills ecosystems to reskill adults

title: European Commission’s new Skills Guarantee: all six pilot projects launched to support workers at risk of unemployment
content: Six pilot projects were selected and launched under the new Skills Guarantee. The pilot has a budget of €14.5 million and will support almost 1,200 European workers at risk of unemployment in the car industry by offering them on-the-job training and new jobs in growing sectors.

title: Natura 2000 Network offers opportunities for sustainable tourism across Europe
content: New guidelines help sites across Europe to integrate tourism and recreational activities while highlighting the growing importance of ecotourism.
snippet: New guidelines help sites across Europe to integrate tourism and recreational activities while highlighting the growing importance of ecotourism.

title: Commission approves €659 million German State aid for four new semiconductor facilities
content: The European Commission has approved €659 million in German State aid to support the establishment of four first-of-a-kind facilities in the semiconductor value chain.
snippet: The European Commission has approved €659 million in German State aid to support the establishment of four first-of-a-kind facilities in the semiconductor value chain.

title: Review finds Foreign Subsidies Regulation fit for purpose, Commission considers targeted changes
content: The European Commission's first review of the Foreign Subsidies Regulation (FSR) has found that the regulation is fit for purpose to address distortions in the internal market caused by foreign subsidies.

title: Commission adopts simpler rules for EU agri-food promotion policy
content: The Commission adopts simplified rules for EU agri-food promotion policy, easing administrative and financial requirements for beneficiaries and Member States.
snippet: The Commission adopts simplified rules for EU agri-food promotion policy, easing administrative and financial requirements for beneficiaries and Member States.

title: EU submits three papers to advance WTO reform discussions
content: Today, the European Union submitted three papers to the World Trade Organization (WTO) on how to reform its work, in support of the new phase of the WTO reform process as discussions among members become more substantive.

title: Questions and answers on the findings of the first review of the Foreign Subsidies Regulation
content: Under Article 52(2) of the Foreign Subsidies Regulation (FSR), the European Commission is obliged to review its practice of implementing and enforcing the rules and present a report to the European Parliament and the Council every three years.

title: Eurostat: Energy efficiency upgrades at home for 24% of people
content: In 2025, 23.9% of the EU population lived in a dwelling in which energy efficiency was improved in the last 5 years.
snippet: In 2025, 23.9% of the EU population lived in a dwelling in which energy efficiency was improved in the last 5 years.

title: BuyCo and Searoutes: Decarbonising container shipping, one booking at a time
content: A container shipment from Shanghai to Paris can produce anywhere from 7.3 to 10 tonnes of CO₂, depending on the route and vessel chosen. Until recently, however, that difference was largely invisible to the companies making shipping decisions.

title: Commission adds exemptions to rules on removability of portable batteries

snippet: The list contains six new categories, including wearable devices such as smartwatches, fitness trackers, and electric toys.

title: Viladecans celebrates a year of Green Leaf action

title: Energy Union Task Force takes stock of oil and gas security of supply in the EU

title: NESBp: strengthening maritime spatial planning across the North Sea and Baltic Sea
content: Today, Europe’s northern seas face growing pressure. The energy transition, climate change, biodiversity loss and increasing geopolitical tensions are reshaping how countries plan and manage their maritime space.
